title: Dicranostigma franchetianum
taxonomy: Dicranostigma franchetianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Papaveraceae. It is native to the Himalayan region and parts of China. This plant is classified as a member of the order Ranunculales and family Papaveraceae. Within the family Papaveraceae, Dicranostigma franchetianum is placed in the subfamily Papaveroideae. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family.
uses: Dicranostigma franchetianum is a plant that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for various purposes. It is valued for its anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, which are believed to help in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant's roots are often used to make medicinal compounds, which are thought to have analgesic and antipyretic effects. Studies have shown that Dicranostigma franchetianum may have potential in the treatment of conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and even certain types of cancer. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.

parts: Dicranostigma franchetianum is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region. side_effects: Dicranostigma franchetianum, also known as Chinese parsley or Chinese celery dropwort, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. There is limited information available on the specific side effects of Dicranostigma franchetianum, but it is generally advised to consult a healthcare professional before using it.
